questions.q: I can't leave the theatre — something won't let me go south.
hints: Why did you come back into this building in the first place?
You're not leaving without your pager. It's still where you left it — down in the basement.
Answer: From the lobby go NW to the Manager's Office, DOWN through the open trapdoor, TAKE PAGER, then UP, SE, and S to leave.

questions.q: I'm in the Basement. Is there anything here besides the pager?
hints: Quite a lot, actually. Examine everything, and take what isn't nailed down.
There's an electrical panel on the wall — and a dark theatre is no friend of yours.
There's a loose page here. Pages are scattered all over the theatre — collecting and reading every one is worth bonus points.
The barrels hold popcorn — and not just one handful. Keep taking it. Trust us, you will want all of it much later.
Answer: TAKE PAGER, GET PAGE, EXAMINE PANEL, TURN ON SWITCH, EXAMINE BARRELS, then GET POPCORN four times (there are four lots).

questions.q: One hook in the Cloakroom is cleaner than the others. What's that about?
hints: A clean hook among cobwebbed ones means it's been used recently. But who has been here?
It isn't for hanging things. Try manipulating it.
Answer: PULL CLEAN HOOK — a secret passage opens, leading down below the theatre. (You won't need to go down there until late in the game, but remember it.)

questions.q: There's a closed metal safe in the Manager's Office. How do I open it?
hints: Examine it — it has a combination dial. Nobody is going to tell you the combination.
Old movies have the answer: safecrackers listen to the tumblers. You need the right instrument for that.
A stethoscope. Have you explored the upper hallways yet? There's a Sick Bay up there with a cabinet worth opening.
Answer: WEAR STETHOSCOPE, LISTEN SAFE, then TURN DIAL LEFT, TURN DIAL RIGHT, TURN DIAL LEFT, TURN DIAL RIGHT. The safe opens — take what's inside, you'll need it.

questions.q: My car's been stolen and a thug with a knife is blocking the street!
hints: You can't win this fight, and you can't run past him. The street is no longer an option.
Let him come. He isn't trying to kill you — he's herding you.
Answer: EXAMINE THUG, then WAIT three times. He drives you back into the theatre — which is exactly where the story wants you. Go N. The real game starts now.

questions.q: My pager just went off! What do I do?
hints: It happens on its own partway through the night — you didn't trigger it and you can't prevent it.
Answer: There's nothing to solve here. Read the message if you're curious, but the beep is just an event — it won't hurt you and you don't need to do anything about it.

questions.q: There's a piano in the Music Room. Can I do anything with it?
hints: Examine it closely — note what it's standing on.
It's on wheels, and it pushes easily.
Where to push it? Think of the piano as furniture — something sturdy to stand on, positioned under something out of reach.
Answer: PUSH PIANO EAST, then PUSH PIANO SOUTH into the Prop Room. STAND ON PIANO, OPEN TRAPDOOR, and go UP into the attic.

questions.q: Is there anything I can do with the chandelier over the auditorium?
hints: You don't reach it — you bring it to you. Machinery for stage rigging is usually kept above.
The machinery is in the attic.
Answer: In the attic, TURN HANDLE. The chandelier is lowered to the auditorium floor. What it's for comes later.

questions.q: Green gas is leaking in the Attic Observatory and it's killing me!
hints: The gas comes from the telescope area. On your first visit, you can dash in and out — don't linger.
Grab the one thing worth taking — the lens — and get out fast.
To stay longer you'll need breathing protection. Theatres keep all sorts of props and costumes...
Answer: There's a gas mask in the Costume Room. WEAR MASK before entering. Permanently stopping the gas comes later, via the telescope itself.

questions.q: What is the telescope in the Observatory for?
hints: It's missing something. Examine it closely.
An ordinary lens isn't enough — it needs to carry a particular pattern before it'll show you anything.
The lens must already be carrying that pattern before it's any use here — getting it there is a separate puzzle. Once it does, wear the gas mask and bring the lens back.

Answer: WEAR MASK, go to the Observatory, PUT LENS IN TELESCOPE (this also stops the gas), then LOOK THROUGH TELESCOPE. You collapse — and wake with a star crystal in your inventory. You will absolutely need it.

questions.q: Rolls of carpet block the passage in the Narrow Hall.
hints: They're just heavy, not magical.
Answer: MOVE CARPET ROLLS. The passage north is freed — and something unpleasant is revealed underneath.

questions.q: I found a corpse under the carpet. Ew. Now what?
hints: Steel yourself and search it. The dead man was here for a reason.
Search the body — he carries an appointment book.
Answer: READ APPOINTMENT BOOK. Reinhart left his cellular phone in the theatre — if you could find it, you could call for help. (And remember where you left the poor fellow.)

questions.q: Is there anything useful in the alley courtyard?
hints: The rubbish bin is closed. Open things.
Answer: OPEN RUBBISH BIN, GET PAGE FROM BIN, GET TICKET FROM BIN. The ticket is your admission to the show — hold onto it.

questions.q: There's a portrait on the staircase landing — is there anything to it?
hints: It is. Get your hands on it — literally.
Answer: FEEL PAINTING, then OPEN DOOR and go N into the Secret Gallery. Take the camera you find there (GET CAMERA) — a flash camera has a very specific use later.

questions.q: What's the deal with the curiously patterned circular window?
hints: First make sure you can see it properly — the curtains on the southern landing are drawn.
Look down — the light through the window casts a patterned spotlight on the lobby floor below.
A pattern made of light... Have you found anything made for catching light? If not, don't go on — explore the attic first and come back.
Answer: Go down to the lobby and PUT LENS IN SPOTLIGHT. The pattern is burned into the lens.

questions.q: A voice at the theatre aisle whispered that I'm "too early" and threw me out!
hints: The ghostly usher takes your ticket but the performance hasn't started. You can't wait it out — time in this place doesn't move on its own.
If time won't pass on its own, you'll need to set it yourself — find something that keeps time.
What you need is hidden close to the lobby, and reaching it is a puzzle in itself.
Answer: With the watch in the booth: PULL DIAL, then TURN DIAL four times until it reads 7:40. Now it's late enough. Return to the aisle with your ticket and DROP TICKET when the voice asks.

questions.q: Is there anything else to find in the Manager's Office?
hints: Quite a bit. Old buildings keep records of their own construction — and offices keep records in furniture.
The Manager's Office desk has a drawer worth opening — there are architect's plans inside.
The plans show the ticket booth lies directly south of the office — and the wall between is just plaster and lath.
Answer: EXAMINE SOUTH WALL, then KICK SOUTH WALL. It gives way, opening an exit south into the booth. Inside: a loose page, and a skeleton with rags and a pocket watch.

questions.q: I'm on the stage/backstage. What should I be doing here?
hints: Backstage, look up — there's a rope, and a door above it.
Climb the rope to the dressing room. The locker there is worth opening — carefully.
Inside the locker: an ancient amulet, a newspaper clipping... and a coiled serpent guarding them.
Don't reach in. The locker itself is your weapon — it's heavy and the stage is below.
Answer: PUSH LOCKER EAST — it falls to the stage and kills the snake. Go D, READ NEWSPAPER, GET AMULET, WEAR AMULET. The amulet matters: it protects you in the underground areas.

questions.q: A shadow creature east of the orchestra pit keeps driving me back!
hints: You can't fight a shadow. But think about what shadows can't survive.
Light — sudden, bright light. Have you found anything that flashes? If not, you'll need it first.
Answer: TAKE PHOTOGRAPH (with the camera from the Secret Gallery). The flash banishes the shadow. You can drop the camera afterwards; continue east, then NW to get under the stage.

questions.q: I can see a western balcony but I can't find any way to walk to it.
hints: Not every gap is crossed on foot.
If the chandelier is still hanging high, you can't cross yet — lower it first. Once it's down: a chandelier on a long chain is a pendulum.
Answer: From the Eastern Balcony, SWING CHANDELIER. You swing across to the Western Balcony — which leads to the back wall and the Secret Library.

questions.q: A huge slug is guarding a nest, and it won't let me near!
hints: Whatever is glinting in that nest, the slug won't let you near it. You need to neutralize the slug without touching it.
Look around the room. What's tall, heavy, and standing right next to it?
The bookcase — look closely. A long chain holds it in place.
Answer: UNHOOK CHAIN, PUSH BOOKCASE — it falls on the slug. Then EXAMINE NEST and GET BLUE PEARL. That strange eye-like pearl will matter a great deal later — keep it safe.

questions.q: A crow on the theatre roof keeps flying away from me — and now it's stolen a key!
hints: You can't catch it. Follow it instead — where did it go?
Chimneys in this theatre connect rooms vertically — the fireplace in the guest star room is a way down.
In the sealed office below, there's a coffin worth opening. The crow steals the key — and drops it further down the chimney.

Answer: Climb down again to the Boiler Room and GET KEY there. That shiny key unlocks a door deep underground later. While you're in the Boiler Room, don't leave empty-handed — there's more here worth taking.

questions.q: What's worth taking in the Boiler Room?
hints: Three things, plus the key the crow dropped. Open and examine everything.
There's a chest worth opening — a bottle of glue inside. Keep it; it solves a problem later.
Look in the coal bin — one lump is not like the others.
Answer: GET KEY, GET BOTTLE (glue, from the chest), GET LUMP, then CLEAN LUMP — it conceals another strange eye-like pearl, violet this time.

questions.q: The mannequins in the Costume Room come alive and shuffle toward me!
hints: They're slow, at least. You don't need to fight them — stop them in their tracks. Literally.
Mannequins have feet — something sticky underfoot would root them in place.
Answer: BREAK BOTTLE (the glue bottle from the Boiler Room chest). The glue splashes across the floor and the mannequins stick fast. Now GET MASK — you'll need it for the gas in the Observatory.

questions.q: There's a locked door in the underground passage below the cloakroom.
hints: You found the way down by pulling the clean hook in the Cloakroom. The door needs a key you may already have.
Remember the crow, the coffin, and the chimney?
Answer: UNLOCK DOOR WITH SHINY KEY (from the Boiler Room), OPEN DOOR, and head E. You can drop the key afterwards.

questions.q: Something huge is growling in a pit and I can't get past it alive.
hints: You cannot fight it and you cannot sneak by. The beast wants to eat — so feed it something.
Feed it something... treated. Did you find a vial of blue liquid in the Sick Bay cabinet? Examine it — read the letter there too.
The liquid is poison. Now, what do you have that a beast would eat? Think of the grimmest item you've encountered. The corpse from under the carpet rolls.
Answer: Bring the corpse and the vial to the pit. POUR LIQUID ON BODY, DROP VIAL, THROW BODY IN PIT. The beast takes the bait, and you can go D and onward.

questions.q: There's a goblin-like creature in a cage deep in the mines. Can it help me?
hints: He's more than he appears, and he knows things nobody else can tell you — particularly about certain strange objects you may have picked up along the way.
Show him things. Note down what he says — his answers are different in every playthrough, and you will need them word for word later.

Answer: SHOW VIOLET PEARL TO GOBLIN, SHOW BLUE PEARL TO GOBLIN, SHOW RED PEARL TO GOBLIN, SHOW GREEN PEARL TO GOBLIN — and WRITE DOWN which socket he names for each. The statue in the Witch's Lair has four sockets, and the correct placement is randomized per game.

questions.q: Rats! The sewer is swarming with rats and I can't get through.
hints: You can't kill that many rats. But rats follow food. Have you been carrying anything edible?
The popcorn from the basement barrels — all four lots. Lay a trail.
Lay the popcorn trail — the western end of the sewer, the center, the eastern end, and the Rat Nest itself. The rats follow it away from where you need to be.

Answer: After laying the trail (W end, center, E end, nest), go NW then W then N, EXAMINE LEVER, WAIT until the rats reach the western end, then PULL LEVER. The sluice gate opens and flushes the rats down the drain.

questions.q: Is there anything worth finding under the stage?
hints: Now that you've made it under the stage, take a careful look around — it's not as empty as it seems.
Among the odds and ends down here, one item is easy to overlook — and it's another of those eye-like pearls.
Answer: From the dark place east of the storage cupboards, go NW to under the stage. EXAMINE ITEMS — among the coin, pencil and paper is the green eye-like pearl. GET GREEN PEARL.

questions.q: What are these strange eye-like pearls actually for?
hints: They're described as eye-like. Something in the deepest part of the theatre is missing its eyes.
The statue in the Witch's Lair — look at its forehead. Four sockets in a diamond.
Each pearl belongs in one specific socket — and the assignment is random in each playthrough. The caged goblin tells you which is which when you show him each pearl.
Answer: PUT each PEARL IN the SOCKET the goblin named for it (e.g. PUT BLUE PEARL IN UPPER SOCKET). When all four are placed, the statue yields a dagger. GET DAGGER, EXAMINE DAGGER.

questions.q: What do I do with this strange dagger?
hints: Look closely at the dagger — the crystal set in it can be removed. And it isn't the only crystal you've come across.
Only one of the two crystals can destroy the evil, and you need to learn which. Remove the dagger's crystal and show both it and the star crystal to the caged goblin, Trent — he can tell them apart.
The coming ceremony is a trap: Elizabeth intends to use the dagger. Make sure the crystal it holds when she takes it is the wrong one for her purpose.

Answer: PUT STAR CRYSTAL IN DAGGER, ASK TRENT ABOUT ELIZABETH, drop everything except the dagger and the tablets from the Sick Bay, then go U, NE, NE, NE and answer YES. Elizabeth takes the dagger and vanishes, releasing you. (You'll recover the dagger and the star crystal afterwards.)

questions.q: I'm outside a burning mansion with a man in pain. How does this end?
hints: Help the man first. You've been carrying something medicinal since the Sick Bay.
Give him the tablets. He is Trent — and he tells you what must be destroyed, and that it is below, in female form.
Take back your tools before descending — the dagger and the star crystal.

Answer: Go D to the wine cellar and THROW STAR AT YOUNG WOMAN. The crystal shatters, the evil is consumed, and you wake in the theatre lobby. Collect your pager from Reinhart in the Manager's Office (NW, D, U), then SE, S, and ENTER CAR. You have won — 50 out of 50.

questions.q: How do I get a perfect score of 50?
hints: Three of the fifty points come from the loose pages scattered everywhere.

There are pages in the basement, hallways, attic, alley bin, secret gallery, reading recess, ticket booth, backstage, under the stage, the underground passages, and the Witch's Lair. Take every one you see.
Answer: Collect every loose page and READ PAGES (read all of them) before the ceremony. Reading the full set is worth 3 points; the rest come from solving the game's puzzles.
